steps.title: Use the right triangle
steps.content:
Triangle BDC is a $30$-$60$-$90$ triangle with hypotenuse $BC=12$. In that special triangle, the side opposite $30^{\circ}$ is half the hypotenuse, so
$$
DC=\frac{12}{2}=6
$$

steps.title: Relate the two halves
steps.content: The same altitude creates triangle ABD on the other side. Since the large triangle is split into matching $30$-$60$-$90$ right triangles, the two halves of the base are equal, so $AD=DC$.

steps.title: Find the requested length
steps.content: Using $AD=DC$ and $DC=6$ gives $$ AD=6 $$ The answer is B.
